observations.recommendation: Prepare certification stating therein that previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the book in support to additional claim of cash advance together with other liquidation documents; otherwise, a grant of additional cash advance shall not be processed and approved.
observations.reason: The required certification in the grant of additional cash advance was not yet being followed.

observations.recommendation: Submit advance copy of procurement documents to COA within the reglementary period to ensure timely review and monitoring of deliveries and inspection purposes.
observations.reason: Submission of contracts and other bid documents is still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
We hereby recommended that management shall undertake the following:
- Ensure that all procurements shall pass through the regular mode of
procurement to promote fair competition in accordance with R.A. 9184.
Comply with the requirements on adequate procurement planning to
forestall emergency purchases.
- Procurement of office supplies and other recurring expenses of the
Municipality shall be undertaken in bulk for a maximum of 3 months
consumption thru procurement service/public bidding or even thru shopping
so as to minimize transactions thru reimbursements.
- Submit a justification letter explaining therein the reasons of the said claims
despite violation of existing regulation.
- Municipal Accountant shall likewise ensure that all disbursements are
supported with complete documentation.

We hereby recommended the following:
- Existing practice of the agency on processing claims not duly supported with
documents shall be discontinued as this is not the proper way of processing
claims and it may further create adverse impact to agency and to concerned
officials if this will continue to exist in their transactions.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for processing
claims without complete documentation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall likewise submit all the required
documentation needed to establish the validity and legality of the
disbursements and henceforth, payment shall only be made for claims
supported with complete documentation.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall render her monthly reports such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls,
Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports
and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th
day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of
accounts.
- The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure prompt submission of DVs and other related reports, undertake
recording in the books of accounts, and submit the same within 10 days of
the ensuing month to the Office of the Auditor.
- Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on
delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free
from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof as required
under article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
- Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining
therein the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate
actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the
delayed/non submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of
the Revised Penal Code.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- To faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports
within the prescribed period. Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize
submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and
immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
- Require concern official/s to submit written justification explaining therein
the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate actions
shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non
submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised
Penal Code.

Submit immediately all the lacking documentations to allow payment for the
acquisition of Motor Vehicle, otherwise, said procurement shall be disallowed in
audit unless sufficiently justified thru submission of a justification letter
explaining therein the reasons for said utilization despite regulations dictate
otherwise.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer shall exert
more efforts to maintain their respective accounting and budget records,
using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly.
- The necessity of additional workforce, who has the required expertise and
qualification, for the said offices shall be properly considered and shall be
prioritized as well including their attendance to trainings/seminars to keep
them abreast of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

The management shall undertake the following:
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in an inventory-taking to comply with the afore-cited regulation.
- Invitation shall be given at least three days prior to the scheduled date to
give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time
inconveniences that may have.

We hereby recommended the following:
- Intensify efforts for the timely conduct of annual physical count of PPE and
submit the required Reports on PPE to COA within deadline set.
- Invite representative from COA to witness the said inventory taking.
Invitation letter shall be sent three (3) days prior to the scheduled date to
give ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may
have.
- Prioritize the update of the property and accounting records including PPE
subsidiary ledger, and immediately present the same to the office of the
auditor for verification and review.

We recommended to management the following:
- Submit all other transaction documents and so we could complete our audit
on GAD Fund and refrain from processing GAD transaction not duly
supported with documents.
- Immediately submit all other requirements in settlement of audit suspension
to avoid disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on
GAD fund as well.

Immediately submit all other documents to settle audit suspension to avoid
disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on LDRRM
fund as well.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required project validation
and ocular inspection could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Settle discrepancies noted in the implementation of the Local Development
Projects and reconcile submitted Program of Work (POW) with the actual
amount utilized per project.

Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant shall process claims/payments not duly supported with required documents. He shall likewise follow correct disbursement system at all times.
observations.reason: in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project implementation during emergency cases.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall update subsidiary ledgers, stock cards and Property Cards, reconcile balances and include ever year the conduct of inventory in the yearly plan; Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may have.
observations.managementAction: Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Inventory and PPE reports yet for submission to COA. Supply and property records and its subsidiary ledgers yet to be presented to COA for validation.

observations.recommendation: Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection could be promptly undertaken as well. Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project implementation during emergency cases.
observations.managementAction: Payments in whatever nature shall be made by check, directly to suppliers/creditors and not thru cash advance in the name of the Municipal Treasurer and/or include the name as payee of the Municipal Treasurer. However, if direct payment is impractical, it may be paid by the disbursing officer in the form of cash out of his cash advance to conform to the above-cited regulation.
observations.reason: Difficulties were encountered in securing other documents for submission to COA due to pandemic.

observations.recommendation: Deduction/withholding and remittances of required taxes specifically on goods and services, and remit the same within the required timeframe which is every 10th of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: Deduction/withholding and remittance of required taxes were undertaken in accordance with BIR laws and regulations.

observations.recommendation: Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare a written justification for the non-settlement thereof, and submit the same to COA for evaluation and review, otherwise, the transactions covered by it will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Majority of audit suspension were already settled.
observations.reason: Other required documents coming from other parties (contractors/suppliers/payees) not yet submitted/presented to us for submission to COA.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the lacking supporting documents are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the Disbursement Voucher.
observations.managementAction: AAR documents of all supporting documents were submitted and the Municipal Accountant submitted all the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of the same on similar transaction.
observations.managementAction: All DVs including supporting documents were already stamped “PAID” all stamped documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of the same on similar transaction.

observations.recommendation: Programs, projects and activities included in the SWM Plan should be implemented gradually depending on the schedule stated in the AA plan. Ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, are in place to give assurance to proper implementation of the plan.
observations.managementAction: Work on the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission is ongoing. Programs, projects and activities gradually implemented depending on the schedule included in the AA plan.
observations.reason: Still waiting for the approval of the plan by the NSWMB, and so revision/adjustments could be made, if there is any.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall also prepare and maintain subsidiary records for LDRRM and make it available to COA for the necessary review and verification; and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Preparation and maintenance of LDRRM registries still on going. Likewise, preparation and updates of LDRRM Subsidiary Ledger (SL) still on going.
observations.reason: Preparation and maintenance of required LDRRM registries still on going. Likewise, preparation and updates of LDRRM Subsidiary Ledger (SL) still on going.

observations.recommendation: Make necessary journal entries to record all agency’s parcels of land and furniture and follow the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17 to be able to correct understatement of PPE account. Identify lots recorded in the books without the required legal documents of ownership and disclose them in the notes to Financial Statements. Continue to work on the titling of the afore-mentioned lots.
observations.managementAction: Ownership documents not yet complete. Majority of agency parcels of land and furniture part complete.
observations.reason: Several lots owned by the agency yet to be recorded in the agency’s books of accounts. Difficulties were encountered in land titling due to non-availability of required land documents.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and technical documentation. Moreover, immediately settle all other related audit suspensions, otherwise, we will be compelled to issue notice of disallowance on items covered by the said suspensions.
observations.managementAction: Procurement and repair and maintenance activities documentation incomplete. Immediate submission to COA was advised.
observations.reason: Lack of awareness on other procurement laws and regulations, hence, other documentation has yet to be prepared for submission to COA. Moreover, immediately settle all other related audit suspensions, otherwise, we will be compelled to issue notice of disallowance on items covered by the said suspensions.

observations.recommendation: Require suppliers to post warranty security on completed deliveries following the prescribed amount and schedule stated in section 62.1 of revised IRR of RA 9184 and provide copy to the auditor immediately afterwards.
observations.managementAction: Warranty security was imposed on several contracted projects and deliveries following the prescribed amount and schedule.
observations.reason: Imposition of warranty security yet to be undertaken on several contracted projects and delivered supplies.

observations.recommendation: Prepare and maintain books for special accounts specifically for the 20% Development Fund through preparation and maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers to comply with the afore-mentioned regulations as well as facilitate monitoring and easy preparation of FS for special accounts. Municipal Accountant should prepare FS for special accounts and shall submit the same to COA as an attachment to the FS for General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Preparation and updates of books for special accounts still on going.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified staff to take charge in the preparation and updates of these special accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments not duly
supported with required documents. He shall likewise follow correct
disbursement system at all times.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and
reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in
the financial statements.
- The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the
required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the
agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and
submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter
shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the
scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle
whatever time inconveniences that may have.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Payments in whatever nature shall be made by check, directly to
suppliers/creditors and not thru cash advance in the name of the Municipal
Treasurer and/or include as payee of the check the name of the Municipal
Treasurer. However, if direct payment to creditor/supplier is impractical, it
may be paid by the disbursing officer in the form of cash out of his cash
advance to conform to the above-cited regulation.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection
could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and
in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall faithfully adhere to the
provisions stated in the afore-mentioned regulations in relation to the
deduction, withholding and remittance of required taxes specifically on goods
and services, and remit the same within the required timeframe which is every
10th of the ensuing month.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare
a written justification for the non-settlement thereof, and submit the same to
COA for the necessary evaluation and review, otherwise, the transactions
covered by it will be disallowed in audit.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made
to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of
documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the
required deadlines.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the required supporting
documents of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A
of the Disbursement Voucher.
- The management shall likewise observe that proper flow of transactions is
strictly followed so that only valid and proper claims are processed and
paid.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs
and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of
the same on similar transaction.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- Submit all lacking documentation to enable us complete our audit on the said
fund and further undertake the required ocular inspection. Moreover, refrain
from processing claims/payments not duly supported with complete
documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Observe deadlines in reports submission for a timely audit and immediate
inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for LDRRM related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.

Recommendations:
- Work on the approval of the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan by the
National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure that programs,
projects and activities included in the plan are in accordance with the National
Solid Waste Management Framework.
- Solid Waste Management programs/projects must align with the approved
SWM Plan. Any deviation from the said plan could be a ground for
disallowance in audit.
- Ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, are in place to
give assurance to proper implementation of the plan.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following measures:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in the
preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers and immediately
provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish
accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivables.
- Upon receipt of the latest Certified List of Taxpayers, the Municipal
Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded
amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted certified list of
taxpayers.
- The accounting office shall likewise maintain an index card for each taxpayer
to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of collectibles from
each taxpayer.
- Regularly update RPT/SET Receivable accounts Subsidiary Ledgers.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to enforce collection strategies and remedies
to increase RPT/SET collections as well as undertake measures to require all
delinquent taxpayers to prioritize payment of tax on real property within the
deadlines and in accordance with existing regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
- Discontinue payment of claims not supported with complete documentations.
- Observe correct disbursement system.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the settlement of all
audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to adhere with the afore-mentioned
regulation on the releases of barangay shares which is within five days after the end of each quarter.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- Budgeted amount in each plan shall be maximized in order to meet its
minimum requirement of implementation.
- Provide our office with the breakdown of expenditures of each plan
especially those implemented by attribution to facilitate the conduct of post-
audit activity.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
- The management shall always formulate GAD Plan with great emphasis
on proper planning and compliance to various GAD policies and
regulations.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for GAD related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit all lacking documentation including un-
submitted Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) to enable us complete our audit
on the said fund.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Comply with the required laws, rules and regulations on LDRRM to
avoid sanction that may be imposed from violating thereof.
- The Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare and maintain separate
LDRRM Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations for
QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay as required in the afore-mentioned
regulation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall also prepare and maintain subsidiary
records for LDRRM and make it available to COA for the necessary
review and verification; and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

Recommendation:
Municipal Budget Officer shall always takes heed to the requirement of the law
and ensure that budget records were updated regularly to be able to provide
reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s Financial
Statements.

Make necessary journal entries to record all agency’s parcels of land and furniture and fixtures worth P611,690.30 in the appropriate PPE account - Identify those lots having uncertain ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes to Financial Statements. - Concerned officials shall continue to work on the titling of the afore-mentioned lots to be able to provide proof of ownership and so the interest of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise, ensures the legitimacy of recording in the books. - Appropriate enough funds for the titling or securing proof of ownership of these parcels of land. - Provide us details of the Furniture, Fixtures and Land account recorded in the books including copy of its updated Subsidiary Ledger (SL).
observations.managementAction: AAR Securing ownership documents Part II still on going.
observations.reason: Several lots owned by the agency yet to be recorded in the agency’s books of accounts. Difficulties encountered in land titling; due to non-availability of required documents.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation in all their Infrastructure Assets. - Make necessary adjusting entries to correct balances of the affected accounts. - Compute depreciation expense and the related accumulated depreciation following the afore-mentioned regulations. - Revisit PPSAS 17 and other pertinent COA regulations, which prescribed accounting treatment for PPE, to keep you guided in the recognition, de-recognition and depreciation of related PPE accounts. - Provide us copy of the updated Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) of all PPE accounts and its Accumulated Depreciation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Part II
observations.reason: Depreciati on was on already in all Infrastructure Assets.

observations.recommendation: Intensify efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure compliance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework. - Implement programs, projects and activities in accordance with the Municipality Solid Waste Management Plan. - Properly document implementation of SWM programs, projects and activities and furnish copy to the office of the auditor together with its breakdown of expenditures to facilitate our audit on Solid Waste Management.
observations.managementAction: AAR Preparatio n of the Ten (10) Part Year Solid Waste Managem ent Plan was already completed, if not for some minor revisions which is likewise to be finished soon.
observations.reason: Revisions still on going

observations.recommendation: Discontinue processing of Local Development Projects without the pre-implementation requirements and other required documents so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance. - Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to immediately settle audit suspensions and so documentation can be forwarded to COA inspectorate team for the necessary inspection and validation. - Submit immediately all the required documentation for inspection and validation, and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.
observations.managementAction: AAR processing of claims/payments for Local Development Projects without the pre-implementation requirements and other required documents so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
observations.reason: Lack of manageme nt awareness on procureme nt updates, specifically on Negotiated Procureme nt submissio n. However, other lacking technical documents were provided by the suppliers/contractor, who refuses to cooperate, and still failed to submit the necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: Concerned official shall refrain from processing claims/payments for LDRRM related programs and activities without proper and complete documentation to avoid audit suspension and disallowance. - Submit LDRRM transaction documents with proper and complete documentation within the required timeframe to facilitate completion of audit and prevent substantial amount of audit suspensions. - Settle all audit suspensions on or before the lapse of the 90 day period of settlement to avoid issuance of a notice of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR Part II
observations.reason: Lacking documents were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the head of agency to instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue certification stating therein that previous cash advance has been liquidated together with other liquidation documents; otherwise, a grant of additional cash advance shall not be processed and approved.
observations.managementAction: AAR Un clear
observations.reason: The municipal accountant overlooked issuance of certificatio n on the grant of additional cash advance. However, they assured us to comply with the recommen dation in year 2020 and onwards.

observations.recommendation: We hereby recommended to the Municipal Mayor and other offices concerned to strictly adhere to the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in all their procurement activities. Moreover, immediately settle all other related audit suspensions, otherwise, we will be compelled to issue notice of disallowance on items covered by the said agency suspensions.
observations.managementAction: AAR Part
observations.reason: Lack of awareness on other procureme nt laws and regulations of the concerned official to work on other related documentat ion has yet to be prepared and submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: The Mayor shall require all department heads/end users to submit their respective PPMP regularly as this will be used as the basis by the BAC in the preparation of the APP, and furnish copy to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter. - The BAC shall include all procurement requirements for the year in the Agency Annual Procurement Plan (APP) to be able to present a complete and valid procurement plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR Revised/Up dated Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2018 was prepared and submitted to COA.
observations.reason: APP already prepared, submitted and thereafter.

observations.recommendation: Review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and accountabilities. - Assigned qualified and efficient staff to assist the accounting unit and in other key offices so that reports can be prepared promptly and within deadlines. - Require payees to submit the required documentation before payment is made. - Strictly comply with the prescribed timeframe on the submission of accounts and financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for violating thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR The management Part has already submitted their reports on time.
observations.reason: Assigned qualified and efficient staff to assist the workforce in the accounting unit and in other key offices and to ensure prompt preparation of reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management and to Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the required copy of procurement contract within the reglementary period to ensure a systematic and effective review process with a view of generating timely and relevant audit results. - Provide us copies of these documents for monitoring of deliveries and timely inspection purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR BAC committee failed to submit the required advanced procurement contract within five (5) days upon perfection, contrary to the regulation.
observations.reason: Failed to submit properly within five (5) days the required procurement contract due to non-availability of other documents to be submitted together with the approved contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to require suppliers to provide warranty security on the completed deliveries following the prescribed warranty amount and schedule stated in section 62.1 of the 2016 revised IRR of RA 9184 and provide copy to the auditor immediately afterwards.
observations.managementAction: AAR Part
observations.reason: Lack of awareness of the processes and regulations on the imposition of warranty security has caused delays in the imposition of the same on contracted projects and delivered supplies.

observations.recommendation: Recommended to management to refrain from awarding labor contract to Pakyaw Group where contract cost exceeds the authorized amount as prescribed in the afore-cited regulation to avoid sanction that may be imposed for violating thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR Grant of Labor Contract to every Pakyaw Group where contract cost exceeds the prescribed limit has been stopped.
observations.reason: Pakyaw Labor Contract was already stopped and complied with the prescribed limit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management shall consider utilization of SEF Fund following the requirement stated in the above law, and likewise submit related transaction documents and other SEF reports exclusive of SEF budget for COA within the required timeframe of submission.
observations.managementAction: AAR Preparatio n and submission of Special Education Fund Expenditures. A follow-up was made.
observations.reason: Disbursement for SEF in CY 2019 was prepared and submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: Instruct concerned official not to process claims for GAD related programs and activities without proper and complete GAD documentation to avoid audit suspension and disallowance. - Require GAD focal point to properly document implementation of GAD programs and activities and submit all the required documentation to the Municipal accountant immediately after program implementation to be able to meet the required timeframe of submission and facilitate completion of audit. - Settle all audit suspensions on or before the lapse of the 90 day period of settlement, otherwise, a notice of disallowance shall be issued.
observations.managementAction: AAR Concerned office has doing their best to comply, and assured to submit lacking documents.
observations.reason: Lack of awareness and the transactions with incomplete supporting documents still exist in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: The municipality shall maintain special accounts in the general fund specifically for the 20% Development Fund through preparation and maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers to comply with the afore-mentioned regulations as well as facilitate monitoring and preparation of FS for special accounts. Municipal Accountant should prepare FS for special accounts and shall submit the same to COA as an attachment to the FS for General Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR Preparatio n and updates of books for special accounts still on going.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified staff to take charge in the preparation and updates of these special accounts.

observations.recommendation:
The following are the recommended measures, viz:
- Make necessary journal entries to record all agency’s parcels of land and
furniture and fixtures worth P611,690.30 in the appropriate PPE account -
General Fund books following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17 to be
able to correct understatement of PPE account balances in the Financial
Statements.
- Identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal
documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes
to Financial Statements.
- Concerned officials shall continue to work on the titling of the afore-
mentioned lots to be able to provide proof of ownership and so the interest
of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise, ensures the
legitimacy of recording in the books.
- Appropriate enough funds for the titling or securing proof of ownership of
these parcels of land.
- Provide us details of the Furniture, Fixtures and Land account recorded in
the books including copy of its updated Subsidiary Ledger (SL).

- The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expense in all their
Infrastructure Assets.
- Make necessary adjusting entries to correct balances of the affected
accounts.
- Compute depreciation expense and the related accumulated depreciation
following the afore-mentioned regulations.
- Revisit PPSAS 17 and other pertinent COA regulations, which prescribed
accounting treatment for PPE, to keep you guided in the recognition, de-
recognition and depreciation of related PPE accounts.
- Provide us copy of the updated Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) of all PPE accounts
and its Accumulated Depreciation.

We recommended to management to undertake the following measures, viz:
- Intensify best efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-
2017 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to
ensure compliance with the National Waste Management Frameworks.
- Implement programs, projects and activities in accordance with the
Municipality Solid Waste Management Plan
- Properly document implementation of the SWM programs, projects and
activities and furnish copy to the office of the auditor together with its
breakdown of expenditures to facilitate our audit on Solid Waste
Management.

- Discontinue processing of claims/payments for Local Development
Projects without the pre-implementation requirements and other required
documents so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to immediately settle audit
suspensions and so technical documentation can be forwarded to COA
inspectorate team for the necessary inspection and validation.
- Submit immediately all the required documentation for inspection and
validation, and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in the
Annual Audit report.

- Concerned official shall refrain from processing claims/payments for
LDRRM related programs and activities without proper and complete
documentation to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Submit LDRRM transaction documents with proper and complete
documentation within the required timeframe to facilitate completion of
audit and prevent substantial amount of audit suspensions.
- Settle all audit suspensions on or before the lapse of the 90 day period of
settlement to avoid issuance of a notice of disallowance.

We recommended to the head of agency to instruct Municipal Accountant to
issue certification stating therein that previous cash advance has been liquidated
and accounted for in the book in support to additional claim of cash advance
together with other liquidation documents; otherwise, a grant of additional cash
advance shall not be processed and approved.

We hereby recommended to the Municipal Mayor and to other offices concerned
to strictly adhere to the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in all their procurement
activities. Moreover, immediately settle all other related audit suspensions,
otherwise, we will be compelled to issue notice of disallowance on items covered
by the said suspensions.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Mayor shall require all department heads/end users to
submit their respective PPMP regularly as this will be used as basis by the
BAC in the preparation of APP, and furnish copy to the office of the
auditor immediately thereafter.
- The BAC shall include all procurement requirements for the year in the
Agency Annual Procurement Plan (APP) to be able to present a complete
and valid procurement plan. Incomplete APP could be a ground for
declaring a null and void procurement activity, thus, should be disallowed
in audit.
- Strictly observe that no procurement shall be authorized and undertaken
unless it is in accordance with the approved APP.

We recommended to management to adopt the following measures:
- Review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of
claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and
accountabilities.
- Assigned qualified and efficient staff to assist the present workforce in
accounting unit and in other key offices so that reports can be prepared
promptly and within deadlines.
- Require payees to submit the required documentation before payment is
made.
- Strictly comply with the prescribed timeframe on the submission of
accounts and financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for
violating thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides
that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof that
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

We recommended to management and to Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
submit the required advance copy of procurement documents within the
reglementary period to ensure a systematic and effective review process with a
view of generating timely and relevant audit results.
We further recommended to concerned persons/offices to provide us copies of
these documents for monitoring of deliveries and inspection purposes.

We recommended to management to require suppliers to post warranty
security on completed deliveries following the prescribed amount and schedule
stated in section 62.1 of revised IRR of RA 9184 and provide copy to the auditor
immediately afterwards.

We recommended to management to refrain from awarding labor contract to
Pakyaw Group where contract cost exceeds the authorized amount as prescribed
in the afore-cited regulation to avoid sanction that may be imposed for violating
thereof.

We recommended that the management shall consider utilization of SEF Fund
following the requirement stated in the above law, and likewise submit related
transaction documents and other SEF reports to COA within the required
timeframe of submission.

The management shall undertake the following, viz:
- Instruct concerned official not to process claims for GAD related
programs and activities without proper and complete documentation to
avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Require GAD focal point to properly document implementation of GAD
programs and activities and submit all the required documentation to the
Municipal accountant immediately after program implementation to be
able to meet the required timeframe of submission and facilitate
completion of audit.
- Settle all audit suspensions on or before the lapse of the 90 day period of
settlement, otherwise, a notice of disallowance shall be issued.

Make necessary journal entries to record all parcels of land, as per reported list of land owned, under Land Account – General Fund books following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17 to be able to correct understatement of Land Account balance in the Financial Statements.
However, identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes to Financial Statements.
The management shall create a committee to fast track titling of the aforementioned parcels of land and/or secure any proof to establish real ownership and so the interest of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise ensure the legitimacy of recording in the books.
Appropriate enough funds for the titling or securing proof of ownership of these lands.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
Refrain from processing claims/payments for GAD related programs and activities with incomplete documentation to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
Moreover, immediately undertake settlement of all audit suspensions as we cannot complete our audit on the said fund without these being settled first.

Recommendation:
Facilitate submission of LDRRM disbursement vouchers with complete and appropriate supporting documents within the deadlines, and consider prompt settlement of LDRRM related audit suspensions for a timely completion of its audit
Management Comment:
The management promised to fast track submission of vouchers and its supporting documents as well as settlement of audit suspensions.

Recommendation:
The LGU shall only include in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) eligible projects for 20% Development Fund as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Otherwise, undertake immediate realignment of these ineligible projects and other unutilized balances to other PPAs within the authorized projects to be able to meet its minimum requirement for 20% Development Fund.
Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to complete the required documentation for 20% Development Fund and submit the same to COA immediately thereafter and so technical documentation can be forwarded to COA inspectorate team for the necessary inspection and validation. Submit all the required documentation for inspection and validation immediately and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.
Discontinue processing of claims and payments for 20% Development Funds without the required pre-implementation requirements and other required documents so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
Provide us copy of the Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash in Bank – LCCA account to confirm unutilized balance of the 20% Development Fund.
Management Comment:
They promised to settle all unsettled suspensions within the 2nd quarter of the year and discontinue processing of claims/payments without the required pre-implementation requirements.

Recommendations:

Make necessary journal entries to record all parcels of land, as per reported list of land owned, under Land Account – General Fund books following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17 to be able to correct understatement of the Land Account balance in the Financial Statements. However, identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes to Financial Statements.

The management shall create a committee to fast track titling of the aforementioned parcels of land and/or secure any proof to establish real ownership and so the interest of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise ensure the legitimacy of recording in the books. 

Appropriate enough funds for the titling or securing proof of ownership of these lands.

           Management Comment:

Management insisted that Tax Declaration and Cadastral Map are enough proof to evidence ownership. However, we requested concerned official to provide us other proof of ownership aside from the two (2) documents that were being presented to satisfy our claim and to prove the legitimacy of recording.

Recommendation:
To ensure that effective check and balance exists, key duties and functions should be assigned to separate individuals. Hire/Designate personnel who possess the required skills and expertise to do the job efficiently. Send them to training for proper orientation and skills development, if necessary.  

Accounting section shall require payee to submit the required documentation before payments are being made.

Instruct officials concerned to submit the disbursement vouchers and other    financial reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 15th day of the ensuing month. 

Discontinue the practice of processing claims/payments without proper and complete documentation.

Strictly observe the correct disbursement system.



     Management Comment:

     The management has promised us to make up for their lapses in reports submission.

We hereby recommended the following:

Disbursement Vouchers on the grant of cash advances shall bear specific legal purpose for which the cash advance was availed of to prevent possible disallowance in audit and be unconstrained from sanction that may be imposed thereof as required under section 128 of P.D 1445.

Prepare Report of Disbursement (ROD) per cash advance to enable us identify specific expenses paid out of that particular cash advance. 

The practice of recording reimbursements in lieu of cash advances shall be discontinued as this manifestly show the motive of circumventing the law and evade such from being taken-up as cash advance.
observations.managementAction: Ensures that no transaction of this nature exists in year 2017 and onwards

observations.recommendation:
The Management shall undertake the following: 

Direct official concerned to limit payment thru reimbursement basis in a manner as required by law to avoid disallowance in audit. 

Comply to the requirement on the principle of imprest system;  that all payment must be paid by check except those that cannot be conveniently paid by check and/or an existence of emergency  or urgently needed circumstances.
observations.managementAction: Claim through reimbursement basis was regulated

observations.recommendation:
Facilitate immediate submission of appointments that are in questioned to avoid negative repercussion, as payment of salary without proper and valid appointment is considered irregular, hence, should be disallowed in audit.

Revisit CSC MC NO. 41s. 1998 or the Revised Omnibus Rules of Appointments and Personnel Action to keep you guided with the hiring and renewal of Plantilla Positions.

Discontinue immediately payment of salary to personnel without proper and valid appointment to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Temporary appointments were already forwarded to CSC for attestation. However, copy of the attested appointment of several personnel yet to be provided to COA

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management shall undertake the following measures:

Limit the hiring of casuals and contractual personnel based on the actual service necessity of the agency’s operation or whose job descriptions and functions are not in duplication and/or can be clearly defined in order to minimize expenditures for personal services.

Consider filling up of existing vacant positions instead hiring of casuals and contractual to perform the work pertaining to a vacant position.

Make revision on the prepared Job Orders following the required format to comply with the above regulations.

Revisit existing polices on hiring if there are any; otherwise formulate the same to ensure compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Hiring of Casuals and Contractual has been regulated

observations.recommendation:
The management shall review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and accountabilities.

Direct all non performing officials to handle their assigned task with diligent and care as well as consider assigning additional personnel to accounting unit and to other key offices whose reports are significant in decision making, and so the reports can be prepared promptly and be able to meet deadlines on report submission, and be unconstrained from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof  as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445. 

Discontinue the practice of processing claims/payments without proper and complete documentation.

Strictly observe the correct disbursement system.
observations.reason: Lack of qualified personnel to help in the preparation of required reports and documentation. However, they had committed to improve their report submission and reconsider increasing their present workforce in order to meet deadlines in reports submission.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Budget Officer shall take heed to the rule of law and consider diligently the importance of maintaining the required Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations using the prescribed forms and update the same regularly.
observations.managementAction: Registries were already prepared and updated

observations.recommendation:
Limit expenditures to what is appropriated in the budget to avoid incurrence of unauthorized expenditures.

Prepare and review carefully the budget so that actual needs of the Municipality are properly addressed and allocated.

Submit a duly approved ordinance/resolution authorizing such realignment and/or modification in the approved budget to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Minimal amount of expenditures still exceeded its budgeted amount. However,   the required ordinance was prepared in order to correct the deficiencies.

observations.recommendation:
Books of Accounts shall be properly maintained and updated regularly and make it available anytime for validation and verification of COA representatives.

The management shall send Municipal Accountant and person in-charge in accounting and bookkeeping functions to accounting related seminars to enhance their knowledge and skills in accounting and bookkeeping.
observations.reason: Preparation and updates are still ongoing. However, they committed to present it to COA for review  the moment it was made available

observations.recommendation: Make necessary adjustment and reclassification entries to correct deficiencies noted to ensure propriety and reliability of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already undertaken to correct deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality GAD Plan shall be formulated following proper planning with utmost consideration on various GAD policies and regulations.

GAD Plan shall be the basis in its implementation, thus, any programs and projects implemented not provided in the GAD Plan are said to be illegal, thus should be disallowed in audit. 

However, a revised GAD Plan may be prepared to suit the present needs of the municipality and the demands of their constituents.

Furthermore, date/information in the GAD accomplishment Report shall tally with actual disbursement to avoid confusion and doubts as to the true intention of the municipality for rendering unreliable information.
observations.reason: Other required documentation still needs to be rendered to COA for audit

observations.recommendation:
A special trust fund account shall be maintained with an authorized depository bank for LDRRM sole purpose only and all unspent LDRRM Fund shall be immediately deposited thereto to comply with the afore-cited regulations.

Discontinue the use of the 30% allocation for Quick Response unless there is a Calamity declaration in your area to avoid possible disallowance in audit.

Moreover, provides us copy of the Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash in Bank – LCAA account as well as duplicate copy of the deposit slip made to a LDRRM account to enable us complete our audit on LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Other required documentation still needs to be rendered to COA for audit

observations.recommendation:
The LGU should only include in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) eligible projects for 20% Development Fund as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Otherwise, undertake immediate realignment of these ineligible projects to other PPAs within the authorized projects as provided in the afore-cited regulations to be able to meet its minimum requirement for 20% Development Fund.

Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to prepare the pre-implementation documents immediately and even before the start of project implementation to ensure meeting of deadlines, as well as avoid difficulties in its preparations as there were documents easily done and complied with less cost and effort if prepared prior to actual implementation. 

Submit all the required documentation for inspection and validation immediately and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.
observations.reason: Other required documentation still needs to be rendered to COA for audit

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct Municipal Accountant and other persons concerned to furnish COA copies of all Contracts, Job Orders and Purchase Orders and other required documents as per COA Circular 2009-001 within the deadline set to give us opportune time in the conduct of necessary audit review and evaluation as well as timely communication of audit results hereof.
observations.reason: Multi-tasking functions.

observations.recommendation:
-Agency Annual Procurement Plan (APP) shall include not only of regular supplies but also of commonly-used equipment and materials to be able to present a complete and valid procurement plan.
-A revision shall be made on the APP to suit the mode of procurement adopted.

-Incomplete APP could be a ground for declaring a null and void procurement activity, thus, should be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Minimal amount of   procurement whose items were not covered by the approved APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned personnel shall prepare a duly accomplished PO, PR and IAR for attachment to the claim for payment to avoid possible suspension in audit. Likewise, RCI and ROD shall be properly numbered and dated for easy reference in audit.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of said reports was undertaken following the required format.

observations.recommendation:
We hereby recommended the following:
Disbursement Vouchers on the grant of cash advances shall bear specific legal purpose for which the cash advance was availed of to prevent possible disallowance in audit and be unconstrained from sanction that may be imposed thereof as required under section 128 of P.D 1445, which states that:
Any violation of the provisions of section 67,68,89,106,108 of this Code or any regulation issued by the Commission implementing these sections, shall be punish by a fine not exceeding one thousand pesos or by imprisonment not exceeding (6) months, or both such find and imprisonment in the discretion of the court.
Prepare Report of Disbursement (ROD) per cash advance to enable us identify specific expenses paid out of that particular cash advance.
The practice of recording reimbursements in lieu of cash advances shall be discontinued as this manifestly show the motive of circumventing the law and evade such from being taken-up as cash advance.
Agency’s Officials including elected officials shall refrain from obtaining cash advances for purposes other than travelling expenses. Payment of operating expenses either for administrative or infrastructure purposes should be made directly to the supplier/contractor and not through cash advance nor reimbursement except for emergency cases as determined by proper authorities wherein cash advances/reimbursements shall be allowed.

The Management shall undertake the following:
Direct official concerned to limit payment thru reimbursement basis in a manner as required by law to avoid disallowance in audit.
Comply to the requirement on the principle of imprest system;  that all payment must be paid by check except those that cannot be conveniently paid by check and/or an existence of emergency  or urgently needed circumstances.

Recommendations:
Facilitate immediate submission of appointments that are in questioned to avoid negative repercussion, as payment of salary without proper and valid appointment is considered irregular, hence, should be disallowed in audit.
Revisit CSC MC NO. 41s. 1998 or the Revised Omnibus Rules of Appointments and Personnel Action to keep you guided with the hiring and renewal of Plantilla Positions.
Discontinue immediately payment of salary to personnel without proper and valid appointment to avoid disallowance in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management shall undertake the following measures:
Limit the hiring of casuals and contractual personnel based on the actual service necessity of the agency’s operation or whose job descriptions and functions are not in duplication and/or can be clearly defined in order to minimize expenditures for personal services.
Consider filling up of existing vacant positions instead hiring of casuals and contractual to perform the work pertaining to a vacant position.
Make revision on the prepared Job Orders following the required format to comply with the above regulations.
Revisit existing polices on hiring if there are any; otherwise formulate the same to ensure compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
The management shall review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and accountabilities.
Direct all non performing officials to handle their assigned task with diligent and care as well as consider assigning additional personnel to accounting unit and to other key offices whose reports are significant in decision making, and so the reports can be prepared promptly and be able to meet deadlines on report submission, and be unconstrained from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof  as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president, any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.
Discontinue the practice of processing claims/payments without proper and complete documentation.
Strictly observe the correct disbursement system.

Recommendation:
The Municipal Budget Officer shall take heed to the rule of law and consider diligently the importance of maintaining the required Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations using the prescribed forms and update the same regularly.

Recommendation:
Limit expenditures to what is appropriated in the budget to avoid incurrence of unauthorized expenditures.
Prepare and review carefully the budget so that actual needs of the Municipality are properly addressed and allocated.
Submit a duly approved ordinance/resolution authorizing such realignment and/or modification in the approved budget to avoid disallowance in audit.

Recommendation:
Books of Accounts shall be properly maintained and updated regularly and make it available anytime for validation and verification of COA representatives.
The management shall send Municipal Accountant and person in-charge in accounting and bookkeeping functions to accounting related seminars to enhance their knowledge and skills in accounting and bookkeeping.

Recommendation:
Make necessary adjustment and reclassification entries to correct deficiencies noted to ensure propriety and reliability of the Financial Statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality GAD Plan shall be formulated following proper planning with utmost consideration on various GAD policies and regulations.
GAD Plan shall be the basis in its implementation, thus, any programs and projects implemented not provided in the GAD Plan are said to be illegal, thus should be disallowed in audit.
However, a revised GAD Plan may be prepared to suit the present needs of the municipality and the demands of their constituents.
Furthermore, date/information in the GAD accomplishment Report shall tally with actual disbursement to avoid confusion and doubts as to the true intention of the municipality for rendering unreliable information.

We have recommended that:
A special trust fund account shall be maintained with an authorized depository bank for LDRRM sole purpose only and all unspent LDRRM Fund shall be immediately deposited thereto to comply with the afore-cited regulations.
Discontinue the use of the 30% allocation for Quick Response unless there is a Calamity declaration in your area to avoid possible disallowance in audit.
Moreover, provides us copy of the Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash in Bank – LCAA account as well as duplicate copy of the deposit slip made to a LDRRM account to enable us complete our audit on LDRRMF.

Recommendation:
The LGU should only include in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) eligible projects for 20% Development Fund as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Otherwise, undertake immediate realignment of these ineligible projects to other PPAs within the authorized projects as provided in the afore-cited regulations to be able to meet its minimum requirement for 20% Development Fund.
Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to prepare the pre-implementation documents immediately and even before the start of project implementation to ensure meeting of deadlines, as well as avoid difficulties in its preparations as there were documents easily done and complied with less cost and effort if prepared prior to actual implementation.
Submit all the required documentation for inspection and validation immediately and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions
Under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.

Stop the payment of purchases out of the cash advance of the municipal treasurer and reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application.

Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered, signed and dated.

We hereby recommended that instruction shall be given to concerned officials to prepare cumulative breakdown of its actual expenditures and submit the same to our office for audit consumption.
Likewise, transaction documents specifically Disbursement Vouchers (DV) shall clearly state specific purpose of its disbursement, hence, facilitate proper monitoring and control.
observations.reason: Budgeted amount for GAD were not maximized, and material amount of GAD transactions were the subject of audit suspensions.

observations.recommendation: Strict compliance with laws, rules and regulations on LDRRMF implementation is hereby enjoined.
observations.reason: Budgeted amount for LDRRM  were not maximized, and material amount of LDRRM transactions were the subject of audit suspensions.

observations.recommendation: The management shall have timely submission of pertinent requirements for inspection and validation to be able to include result of inspection/validation in the Annual Audit Report.
observations.reason:
Several projects were already inspected and validated by the COA inspectorate team.

However, we have noted  several non-eligible expenditures in 2015  and 2016

observations.recommendation:
-Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the Office of the Auditor immediately thereafter.
-Conduct and complete the Annual Physical Count on Non-Expendable Property to comply with the above-mentioned requirement and determine compliance with the following management assertions, viz:
-Physical existence of the assets which includes determination and classification of obsolete, unserviceable and/or condemnable assets.
-Correct valuation of the assets
-Exert more efforts in locating old property records on acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of Subsidiary Ledgers
-Maintain/provide Subsidiary Ledgers (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC) of PPE accounts and reconcile balances of the SL with the Property Cards and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balances in the Financial Statements.
-Property tag shall be present in every item in the PPELC for easy identification of PPE for disposal, transfers and the like
-Issue ARE/PAR to every officials and employees having property accountability and renew the same every three years or every time there is a change in accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct Municipal Accountant and other persons concerned to furnish COA copies of all Contracts, Job Orders and Purchase Orders and other required documents as per COA Circular 2009-001 within the deadline set to give us opportune time in the conduct of necessary audit review and evaluation as well as timely communication of audit results hereof.
observations.reason: Multi-tasking functions

observations.recommendation:
-Agency Annual Procurement Plan (APP) shall include not only of regular supplies but also of commonly-used equipment and materials to be able to present a complete and valid procurement plan.
-A revision shall be made on the APP to suit the mode of procurement adopted. 

-Incomplete APP could be a ground for declaring a null and void procurement activity, thus, should be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: There were still  procurement undertaken whose items were not covered by the approved APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned personnel shall prepare a duly accomplished PO, PR and IAR for attachment to the claim for payment to avoid possible suspension in audit. Likewise, RCI and ROD shall be properly numbered and dated for easy reference in audit.
observations.reason: Several reports were prepared by the requesting office who does not know correct processes of accomplishing the said reports

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant shall see to it that Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements submitted by the Municipal Treasurer were supported with  complete disbursement vouchers together with its supporting documents, and liquidation documents, respectively, before forwarding the same to the office of auditor for necessary examination and audit.

-Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued.
observations.reason: Multi-tasking functions among concerned officials  is the primary reason.

observations.recommendation:
Direct all non-performing officials and employees to report to their respective offices and perform their individual work assignments,  so as to minimize workloads perform by other agency officials which have caused delays in the submission of reports.
Cause the prompt submission of the disbursement vouchers and other required reports to be able to have timely audit of transactions and communication of result thereof and be unconstrained from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof  as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
observations.reason: Multi-tasking functions among concerned officials  is the primary reason.
